name: Mycetoma (Madura Foot)
description: A chronic, progressive granulomatous subcutaneous tissue disease caused by fungi or bacteria. Typically affects feet of barefoot farmers, forming multiple sinuses that discharge purulent fluid and fungal grains.

climateLinks.impact: Fungi live in thorny shrubs. Spores enter subcutaneous tissues through punctures from acacia thorns or agricultural splinters.

prevention: Wearing protective leather boots in fields
Immediate cleaning and antiseptic care for thorn pricks
Aggressive surveillance of rural farming populations
triageAdvisory: Characterized by painless woody swelling, multiple discharging sinuses, and black/yellow granular discharge. Run tissue biopsy and culture. Eumycetoma requires long-term Itraconazole; Actinomycetoma requires prolonged combined antibiotics.
